# Architecture

How mannn-hestia-proxy templates work internally.

## Template File Types

HestiaCP web templates live in `/usr/local/hestia/data/templates/web/nginx/php-fpm/`.

Each template consists of 3 files:

### `.tpl` ‚Äî HTTP Server Block

Nginx config for HTTP (port 80). HestiaCP replaces `%variables%` before writing to disk.

```nginx
server {
    listen      %ip%:%web_port%;
    server_name %domain_idn% %alias_idn%;

    # Include proxy config generated by .sh
    include %home%/%user%/conf/web/%domain%/nginx.proxy.conf;

    # Standard HestiaCP includes
    include %home%/%user%/conf/web/%domain%/nginx.conf_*;
}
```

Available variables:
| Variable | Replaced With |
|----------|--------------|
| `%ip%` | Server IP |
| `%web_port%` | HTTP port (usually 80) |
| `%web_ssl_port%` | HTTPS port (usually 443) |
| `%domain_idn%` | Domain name (IDN encoded) |
| `%alias_idn%` | Domain aliases |
| `%user%` | HestiaCP username |
| `%home%` | Home directory base (usually `/home`) |
| `%docroot%` | Document root path |
| `%ssl_pem%` | SSL certificate path |
| `%ssl_key%` | SSL private key path |

### `.stpl` ‚Äî HTTPS Server Block

Same as `.tpl` but with SSL directives. Includes `nginx.proxy.ssl.conf` instead of `nginx.proxy.conf`.

### `.sh` ‚Äî Shell Script (runs on template apply)

Executed by HestiaCP as **root** when the template is applied or re-applied.

Receives 5 positional arguments:
| Arg | Variable | Example |
|-----|----------|---------|
| `$1` | user | `myuser` |
| `$2` | domain | `api.example.com` |
| `$3` | ip | `203.0.113.10` |
| `$4` | home | `/home` |
| `$5` | docroot | `/home/myuser/web/api.example.com/public_html` |

**Important**: `%variables%` are NOT available in `.sh` files. Use positional args only.

## What .sh Does (execution order)

```
1. Create app directory        ‚Üí mkdir -p, chown, chmod
2. Create default .env         ‚Üí runtime-specific safe default port
3. Create placeholder code     ‚Üí server.js / main.go / app.py (if not exists)
4. Go: auto-compile            ‚Üí go build -o server (if main.go exists)
5. Validate PORT from .env     ‚Üí allow only runtime-specific safe port range
6. Generate nginx proxy config ‚Üí nginx.proxy.conf + nginx.proxy.ssl.conf
7. Create unique systemd service ‚Üí /etc/systemd/system/mannn-{user}-{hash}.service
8. Enable + start service      ‚Üí systemctl enable + start (or restart)

Docker backend mode is the exception: it only writes nginx proxy files from `BACKEND_PORT` and does not create a Docker service.
```

## Dynamic Proxy Pattern

Traditional approach: one template per port (static).
```
template-nodejs-3100.tpl ‚Üí proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3100
template-nodejs-3101.tpl ‚Üí proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3101
```

mannn-hestia-proxy approach: one template, port from .env (dynamic).
```
template applies ‚Üí .sh reads .env PORT ‚Üí generates include file ‚Üí nginx loads it
```

The `.tpl`/`.stpl` use `include` to load a generated config file:
```nginx
include %home%/%user%/conf/web/%domain%/nginx.proxy.conf;
```

This file is generated by `.sh` with the correct port from `.env`.

## File Locations

After template is applied to domain `api.example.com` for user `myuser`:

```
/home/myuser/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ conf/web/api.example.com/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nginx.conf              ‚Üê main nginx config (HestiaCP managed)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nginx.proxy.conf        ‚Üê HTTP proxy include (generated by .sh)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ nginx.proxy.ssl.conf    ‚Üê HTTPS proxy include (generated by .sh)
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ web/api.example.com/
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ private/
        ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ nodejs/              ‚Üê app directory
            ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .env             ‚Üê PORT=3100
            ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ server.js        ‚Üê app code

/etc/systemd/system/
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ mannn-myuser-a1b2c3d4e5f6.service  ‚Üê auto-created systemd service
```

## Generated Systemd Service

Naming: `mannn-{user}-{hash(domain)}` to prevent collisions between similar domain strings.

Example for user `myuser` and `api.example.com` ‚Üí `mannn-myuser-a1b2c3d4e5f6.service`:

```ini
[Unit]
Description=Node.js app for api.example.com
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=myuser
Group=myuser
WorkingDirectory=/home/myuser/web/api.example.com/private/nodejs
ExecStart=/usr/local/node/bin/node server.js
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5
Environment=NODE_ENV=production
Environment=PORT=3100

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

## Proxy Headers

All proxy configs pass these headers to the backend app:

| Header | Value | Purpose |
|--------|-------|---------|
| `Host` | `$host` | Original domain name |
| `X-Real-IP` | `$remote_addr` | Client's real IP |
| `X-Forwarded-For` | `$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for` | Proxy chain IPs |
| `X-Forwarded-Proto` | `$scheme` / `https` | Original protocol |
| `Upgrade` | `$http_upgrade` | WebSocket support |
| `Connection` | `"upgrade"` | WebSocket support |

Timeouts: connect/send/read all set to 60s.

## Security Headers

All templates include these response headers applied at the nginx layer:

| Header | Value | Scope |
|--------|-------|-------|
| `X-Frame-Options` | `SAMEORIGIN` | All templates |
| `X-Content-Type-Options` | `nosniff` | All templates |
| `X-XSS-Protection` | `1; mode=block` | All templates |
| `Referrer-Policy` | `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` | All templates |
| `Content-Security-Policy` | `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self'; font-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none';` | All templates |
| `Permissions-Policy` | `camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), payment=()` | All templates |
| `Strict-Transport-Security` | `max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload` | SSL templates only |
| `proxy_hide_header` | `X-Powered-By` | All templates ‚Äî prevents backend technology disclosure (e.g. `PHP/8.x.x`) |

## Re-applying Template

When you re-apply a template (`v-change-web-domain-tpl`):

- `.sh` runs again from scratch
- `.env` ‚Äî **preserved** if already exists
- App code ‚Äî **preserved** if files exist (placeholder only created when no files found)
- nginx proxy config ‚Äî **regenerated** from current .env PORT
- Systemd service ‚Äî **regenerated** and restarted (or started if new)
- Go binary ‚Äî **rebuilt** only if no binary exists and main.go exists

This means re-applying is safe ‚Äî it won't overwrite your app code.


## Security Hardening

All runtime templates source a shared helper file `mannn-security.sh` during installation. The helper provides:

- collision-safe service/container naming based on `user + sha256(domain)`
- blocked-port enforcement for control-plane and common infrastructure ports
- per-runtime localhost port allowlists
- basic symlink rejection before root writes files

### Docker hardening

The Docker template no longer runs `docker compose up -d --build`, `docker build`, `docker pull`, or `docker run` against user-writable files. It only reads a localhost backend port from `.env` and writes nginx proxy configuration:

```
BACKEND_PORT=9100
```

Your container or compose stack must be started separately and listen on `127.0.0.1:BACKEND_PORT`.
